title: Star Wars Missions 7: Ithorian Invasion
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:Missions7.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 7:
Ithorian Invasion'' |author=[[Dave Wolverton]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[March]] [[1998]]''[[Star Wars Missions 7: Ithorian Invasion]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=71 |isbn=0590120476 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]The date of this story is established internally in relation to {{Film|IV}}, which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Missions 6: The Search for Grubba the Hutt]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Missions 8: Togorian Trap]]'' }} '''''Star Wars Missions 7: Ithorian Invasion''''' is the seventh adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[March]] [[1998]]. {{Book-stub}} ==Publisher's summary== ''Bounty hunters have taken Grubba the Hutt to the planet Ithor. You must get him back. The Ithorian leader Momaw Nadon has offered to help you. But in return you must aid him in his revolt against the Empire. Imperial forces have taken control of Ithor's center, the ''Tafanda Bay''. Your Mission: to liberate the Ithorians—and find Grubba the Hutt—before it is to late.'' ''May the Force be with you.'' ==Plot summary== [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], approaches [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]], hoping to reclaim possession of [[Grubba Desilijic Aarrpo|Grubba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. His passengers are [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], [[Momaw Nadon/Legends|Momaw Nadon]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. Attempting to evade [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] detection, he modifies the ''Falcon''{{'s}} transponder to transmit the data of a diplomatic shuttle, while masquerading as the ship of [[Master Torturer]] Sir [[Vengnar Heiff]]. The Imperials allow them to approach the ''[[Tafanda Bay]]'', a herdship that resides above Ithor's surface. Meanwhile, on the ''Tafanda Bay'', [[General/Legends|General]] [[Olan Dewes]] gives [[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]], [[Eron Stonefield]], and [[Udin]] a tour of some botanical gardens. The three bounty hunters have captured Grubba, and are keeping him safe on the herdship. Feigning a crash so the Imperials will not be surprised when Heiff fails to appear, Solo sets the ''Falcon'' down at the [[Nadon Shrine]]. There, Nadon is reunited with his wife, [[Fandomar Nadon|Fandomar]], and his son, [[Do-Forow Nadon|Do-Forow]]. Before allowing Solo and his companions to hunt for Grubba, however, Nadon insists that they consult [[The Oracle (Ithorian)|The Oracle]]. The Oracle tells the group of the plight of the Ithorians, before sending them to the ''Tafanda Bay''. Meanwhile, on the herdship, Dewes receives word that the ''real'' Heiff will be coming to Ithor to interrogate Ithorian prisoners. One of the Rebels is selected to accompany Nadon to the ''Tafanda Bay''. After spending the night in the jungle, the pair encounter several dangers, including Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Eventually, they board the ''Bay'', where they are confronted by Ithorians sympathetic to the Empire, led by [[Boma Inondo]]. The Rebel knocks the Ithorian into a pile of berries, while the other Ithorians relent, and explain that Sir Vengnar Heiff is on board. Planning to raid the Imperial armory and start an uprising on the ''Bay'', the Rebel and the Ithorians encounter an [[IG-88 assassin droid/Legends|IG-88 assassin droid]], which they destroy. Taking the Imperial weapons, the Ithorians engage the Imperials, beginning the [[First Battle of Ithor]]. Seizing the control room, General Dewes is captured, and the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] enters the system, driving off the Imperial presence. The Rebel then attempts to find Sir Heiff, in the prison bays. Combating the Master Torturer, the Rebel incapacitates Heiff, and leaves him to the mercy of the Ithorians. The Rebel then encounters three bounty hunters, who flee the ''Bay'' with what seems to be a sack containing Grubba. Fortunately, it only contained Grubba's dinner, and the young Hutt is found safe and alive in one of the herdship's staterooms. Solo contacts Jabba, requesting that he meet him on [[Togoria/Legends|Togoria]] to negotiate the transfer of Grubba. The Hutt crimelord agrees, but secretly informs Dengar of their rendezvous, ordering him to kill Solo upon arrival. ==Release and reception== *''Ithorian Invasion'': {{ISBN|0-590-12047-6}} *Star Wars
